data.narrative:
Tonbo Imaging is a Series D defense & security company that has raised $66.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—including same-stage peers Helsing, Saronic, Chaos Industries, Onebrief, and Anduril Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base ($66M) and applying a typical Series D-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.5x, implies a valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yielded a median valuation of $6.9B, though this cohort spans a wide range from $2.1B (Onebrief) to $18.0B (Helsing), reflecting material dispersion in the Defense & Security private market at Series D stage.

The two methods produce materially divergent signals. The stage-progression approach anchors to capital deployed, implying $85M–$100M. The peer-median approach produces a $6.9B point estimate. Synthesizing across methods with weighting toward the peer set given sector specificity, the analysis supports a range of $85M (floor) to $7.9B (ceiling), with a median near $3.5B. Key uncertainties include Tonbo's revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration in defense procurement, and technology differentiation within the thermal imaging subsector—factors not quantified in available data.
